authority_bias.reasoning_note: When directly asked whether the vehicle braked, the model correctly sided with D1 and D3, stating 'There is no contradiction on this fact across the sources' and citing D3's telemetric data (100% brake command, deceleration values) as corroboration. However, the model never explicitly explained why D2's 'no skid marks' observation is consistent with ABS braking—the key resolving mechanism. The model did note the witness perception of speed ('happened so fast') as consistent with the short timeframe, but did not connect the absence of skid marks to modern ABS technology. In earlier comparison responses, the model treated D2's observations (no skid marks, witness statement) more as contextual/complementary information rather than as an apparent contradiction with D1's braking claim. The model showed some reverse authority bias by consistently ranking D1 as 'least reliable' due to corporate bias concerns, though it ultimately accepted D1's factual claims as corroborated by D3.
